Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had 51 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 423 upvotes, 109 comments
  3. An Example of Statistical Investigation of the Text Eugene Onegin – Markov, 1913 [pdf] 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 294 upvotes, 74 comments
  5. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 290 upvotes, 86 comments
  6. Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise LiteLLM 127 upvotes, 36 comments
  7. Emacs-libgterm: Terminal emulator for Emacs using libghostty-vt 63 upvotes, 15 comments
  8. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 236 upvotes, 84 comments
  9. LinkedIn is illegally searching your computer 1143 upvotes, 527 comments
  10. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 187 upvotes, 73 comments
  11. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 169 upvotes, 72 comments
  12. EmDash: A Fresh Take on CMS 30 upvotes, 8 comments
  13. Significant Raise of Reports 216 upvotes, 102 comments
  14. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 563 upvotes, 297 comments
  15. Pam Bondi ousted as Attorney General 30 upvotes, 9 comments
  16. Renewables reached nearly 50% of global electricity capacity last year 100 upvotes, 44 comments
  17. Snow melt-off in American west stuns scientists 61 upvotes, 24 comments
  18. Delve allegedly forked an open-source tool and sold it as its own 163 upvotes, 79 comments
  19. Gone (Almost) Phishin' 139 upvotes, 67 comments
  20. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 91 upvotes, 42 comments
  21. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 159 upvotes, 94 comments
  22. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 226 upvotes, 146 comments
  23. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 150 upvotes, 93 comments
  24. Show HN: I built a DNS resolver from scratch in Rust – no DNS libraries 64 upvotes, 43 comments
  25. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 18 upvotes, 8 comments
  26. Quadratic Micropass Type Inference 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  27. Reinventing the pull request 64 upvotes, 51 comments
  28. A forecast of the fair market value of SpaceX's businesses 67 upvotes, 56 comments
  29. Even GPT-5.2 Can't Count to Five: Zero-Error Horizons in Trustworthy LLMs 22 upvotes, 19 comments
  30. Telli (YC F24) is hiring engineers, designers, and more (on-site, Berlin) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 2:00am, 3 April 2026. Source here.
